title: Trisetum alopecuros
taxonomy: Trisetum alopecuros is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It belongs to the genus Trisetum, a group of grasses that are native to various parts of the world. Within the genus Trisetum, T. alopecuros is often placed in the subgenus Elymus, which is characterized by its distinctive growth habits and leaf morphology. Further classification within the subgenus is not well established, and the species is often studied in relation to its ecological and taxonomic characteristics. The exact classification of T. alopecuros may vary depending on the source and the specific classification system used.

intro: Trisetum alopecuros, also known as Alopecuros Grass or Meadow Grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues.
